growth_records.strain: Enrichment culture (26 MAGs; no isolate strain)

up.method: chemostat mass balance: D*(S_feed-S_reactor)=0.5/day*(5 mM-0)=2.5 mmol/L/day=0.104 mmol/L/h; acetate fully consumed during oxic phases
D*(S_feed)=0.5/day*2 mM=1.0 mmol/L/day=0.0417 mmol/L/h; nitrate mostly used up in both phases

growth_records.cite: Li et al. Frequency of change determines effectiveness of microbial response strategies. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01515-9. [PMC10579261]
growth_records.snippet: Fresh medium was pumped ... at a rate of 0.5 l per day (one volume change per 2 days) ... a dilution rate of 0.5 volume changes per day. This corresponds to a doubling time of 1.4 days. For high-frequency experiments, each phase lasted for
growth_records.notes: Community-level (not a single isolate). Growth rate = chemostat dilution rate at steady state: D=0.5 volume changes/day=0.5/day => mu=0.5/day/24=0.0208 /h. Doubling time reported as 1.4 days (=33.3 h); check ln2/0.5=1.386 day=33.3 h. 'Almost all populations maintained a steady growth rate under both redox conditions.' Uptake computed volumetrically from chemostat mass balance (feed 5 mM acetate, 2 mM NO3, D=0.5/day); cannot normalize to gDW (DNA used only as biomass proxy). High-frequency treatment: sulfate near-constant (mean 4.9 mM), sulfide undetectable in oxic phases => no net sulfur secretion. pH set to 6.5-7.5 midpoint (7.0). Temperature: room temperature, exact value not stated.

carbon_growth.strain: Enrichment culture (26 MAGs; no isolate strain)
carbon_growth.citation: Li et al. Frequency of change determines effectiveness of microbial response strategies. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01515-9. [PMC10579261]
